findings.evidence: `Subcomodule.lean:45` and `Comodule.lean:45-46` define the existing API with `[AddCommMonoid C/M]`; `Comodule/Preadditive.lean:17` documents deriving additive groups by `Module.addCommMonoidToAddCommGroup`, and `Preadditive.lean:206-220` uses local `letI` for `C M N P`.
findings.fix: State the public variables with `[AddCommMonoid C]`, `[AddCommMonoid M]`, `[AddCommMonoid N]` (and similarly `P` in `comap_comap`), then introduce local `letI : AddCommGroup _ := Module.addCommMonoidToAddCommGroup R` only in proofs/definitions that call additive-group exactness lemmas.
findings.issue: The file-level public assumptions `[AddCommGroup C]`, `[AddCommGroup M]`, and `[AddCommGroup N]` are stronger than TauCeti’s `Comodule`/`Subcomodule` convention over a `CommRing`; over a ring these groups are derivable from `[AddCommMonoid _] [Module R _]`, so users of the existing monoid-level comodule API cannot use these declarations without extra instances.

summary: The comap/kernel API is mathematically at the right flat ring level, but its public signatures unnecessarily require explicit additive-group instances instead of deriving them from the ring-module structure as the existing comodule API does.
